content:
- Final closeout completed: task_switch ran, all changes were staged, and commit e205316 was created with the required task-finished message plus gate evidence and implementation details.
- The post-commit docs publish hook ran successfully and also pushed the mdBook sync commit 0550692a to the separate pgtuskmaster-docs repo.
- Next step is just the final git push for the main repo and then report completion.

- After the follow-up push, the worktree still showed Ralph current-task bookkeeping files modified again. I am inspecting those diffs now and will capture them in one last cleanup commit if they are real file changes.

- Read progress log plus `.ralph/current_tasks.md` and `.ralph/current_tasks_done.md` to choose the next task strictly by Ralph precedence.
- Found exactly one active candidate in `.ralph/current_tasks.md`: `.ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-docker-helper-ignored-errors.md` with `<passes>false</passes>`. Checked the task file and confirmed no `<blocked_by>` tags, so it is unblocked and must be selected under the bug-first rule.
- Wrote `.ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-docker-helper-ignored-errors.md` to `.ralph/current_task.txt`. Next agent should execute that bug task and validate `make check`, `make test`, and `make lint`.

- Read previous Ralph progress and .ralph/current_task.txt.
- Current task points to .ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-docker-helper-ignored-errors.md.
- Reading the task file now to determine whether this turn should plan, verify plan, or execute.

- Task file is in initial state with no TO BE VERIFIED / NOW EXECUTE marker.
- Entered planning phase and started reading tools/docker/common.sh and tools/docker/smoke-cluster.sh.
- Need a detailed plan that removes ignored-error patterns and preserves diagnostics, then write it back to the task file with TO BE VERIFIED and stop.

- Confirmed execution scope is broader than the task examples: tools/docker/smoke-single.sh still has compose_down ... || true.
- Reading docs and tests now so the task plan explicitly covers documentation updates and verification gates.
- Need the plan to be useful for the next engineer, not just a loose edit list.

- Finished the planning pass for .ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-docker-helper-ignored-errors.md.
- Wrote a detailed execution plan into the task file, expanded scope to include tools/docker/smoke-single.sh, and ended the plan with TO BE VERIFIED.
- Important findings for the verifier: cluster.sh already models the stricter teardown contract, smoke-single still fully swallows compose_down failure, and wait_for_ha_member_count needs richer final diagnostics without noisy retry logs.
- Stop here. Next turn must do the skeptical verification pass, alter at least one thing in the plan, and only then replace TO BE VERIFIED with NOW EXECUTE.

- Read Ralph progress log and confirmed the current task is .ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-docker-helper-ignored-errors.md.
- This turn is not fresh planning anymore; the log says the prior turn ended with TO BE VERIFIED and highlighted stricter teardown and diagnostics concerns.
- Reading the task file now for the skeptical verification pass. I need to change at least one thing in the plan before promoting it to NOW EXECUTE.

- Performed the skeptical verification pass on .ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-docker-helper-ignored-errors.md.
- Verified the actual scripts: smoke-single still uses compose_down >/dev/null 2>&1 || true, smoke-cluster logs a generic warning, and common.sh wait_for_ha_member_count still collapses transport/parse/not-ready failures.
- Changed the plan materially before approval: the cleanup contract now explicitly requires each smoke script to preserve incoming exit status in its EXIT trap and use a shared helper only for captured teardown diagnostics.
- Also tightened the verification section to include bash -n on edited scripts before the full make gates.
- Replaced TO BE VERIFIED with NOW EXECUTE and must stop here immediately so the next turn can execute the approved plan without re-exploration.

- Loaded previous Ralph progress and resolved current task path to .ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-docker-helper-ignored-errors.md.
- Prior turn already completed skeptical plan review and promoted the task file to NOW EXECUTE, so this turn should execute without fresh exploration.
- Reading the task file contents now to confirm the exact checklist and then I will implement the approved plan, run all required gates, update docs, and complete Ralph handoff steps.

- Confirmed NOW EXECUTE state in the task file and limited execution scope to tools/docker/common.sh, tools/docker/smoke-single.sh, tools/docker/smoke-cluster.sh, plus docs only if behavior wording is stale.
- Current code still matches the approved plan: smoke-single swallows compose_down completely, smoke-cluster logs a generic warning, and wait_for_ha_member_count conflates curl/parse/not-ready failures.
- Patching shared cleanup diagnostics and HA timeout reporting next, then I will run targeted shell validation before the full make gates.

- Patched tools/docker/common.sh, tools/docker/smoke-single.sh, and tools/docker/smoke-cluster.sh.
- Added explicit compose teardown diagnostics, preserved incoming trap exit status in both smoke cleanup handlers, and made HA readiness timeouts report the last concrete failure reason/detail.
- bash -n passes on all edited scripts. Docs spot-check found no stale statements about the old swallowed-error behavior, so proceeding to the required full make gates.

- make check and make test both passed.
- make test-long is currently running the Docker validation path; compose-config passed and the single-node smoke brought the stack up cleanly before the gate went quiet for a long interval.
- Inspecting live subprocess state in parallel now to distinguish a legitimately long cluster scenario from a hung gate before deciding whether any further intervention is needed.

- make test-long exposed a real blocker outside the original cleanup changes: wait_for_sql_ready could hang forever inside one docker compose exec attempt, causing the entire smoke gate to time out after 600s.
- Patched tools/docker/common.sh so each SQL readiness probe is wrapped with timeout, records the last failure reason, and lets the outer polling loop continue retrying instead of hanging on one stuck exec.
- Cleaned up the timed-out smoke-single Compose project manually and am rerunning the full required gate sequence from the top against the final code state.

- Root cause confirmed: docker compose exec hangs in this environment, but docker exec against the resolved container ID works immediately for the same psql probe.
- Updated wait_for_sql_ready to resolve the service container with docker compose ps -q and then run the bounded SQL probe via docker exec with PGPASSWORD; also passed the real superuser secret into smoke-cluster.
- Verified the fix with a direct ./tools/docker/smoke-single.sh run, which now passes cleanly. Restarting the full required gate sequence so the final evidence reflects the finished code.

- Another long-gate blocker surfaced after the SQL fix: check_etcd_health and the cluster replication probe still used docker compose exec, and smoke-single hung there after SQL readiness succeeded.
- Refactored the remaining helper probes to resolve service container IDs via docker compose ps -q and execute commands with docker exec instead; also threaded the real superuser password through cluster.sh and wait_for_cluster_replication_roles.
- The stale smoke-single project from the aborted long gate has been torn down cleanly. Restarting the full gate sequence again from the top against the final helper set.

- Final validation succeeded on the finished code: make check, make test, make test-long, and make lint all passed.
- Updated the bug task file to completed with <passes>true</passes> and checked acceptance criteria.
- Running .ralph/task_switch.sh now, then I will stage all changes including .ralph bookkeeping, commit with the required task-finished message, and push.
